title: Tightening energy-based boson truncation bound using Monte Carlo-assisted methods
authors: Jinghong Yang, Christopher F. Kane, Shabnam Jabeen
abstract: Quantum simulation offers a promising framework for quantum field theory calculations. Obtaining reliable results, however, requires careful characterization of systematic uncertainties. One important source is the boson truncation error, which arises from representing infinite-dimensional local Hilbert spaces with finite-dimensional ones. Previous studies have examined this problem from several perspectives. In particular, Jordan, Lee, and Prekill (arXiv:1111.3633) derived an energy-based bound applicable to generic low-energy states across a broad class of field theories. However, this approach often yields overly conservative bounds, especially at large volumes. In this work, we introduce a new methodology that significantly tightens the energy-based boson truncation bound through two complementary advances: an improved analytic derivation and a Monte Carlo-based numerical procedure. We demonstrate the method in (1+1)-dimensional scalar field theory and (2+1)-dimensional U(1) gauge theory in the dual formalism. Our approach substantially mitigates the volume dependence of the required truncation cutoff, achieving reductions nearly proportional to the volume in some cases and to the square root of the volume in others.
summary: This paper addresses the inefficiency of current methods for determining the truncation cutoff needed when simulating bosonic fields on quantum computers. By using a combination of a new analytical p-norm technique and Monte Carlo simulations, the authors significantly tighten the energy-based bounds. This reduction in the required cutoff helps mitigate the unfavorable scaling of computational resources with system volume. The approach is successfully applied to both scalar field theory and U(1) gauge theory.
main_problem: The existing energy-based bound for boson truncation in quantum field theory simulations is overly conservative and scales unfavorably with the lattice volume, leading to excessive qubit requirements.
main_result: The authors developed a new methodology that significantly tightens the truncation bound, achieving reductions in the required cutoff proportional to the volume or its square root in different field components.
method: A dual approach combining an improved analytical derivation (p-norm trick) with a numerical procedure using Euclidean path-integral Monte Carlo (Monte Carlo-assisted method).
model_or_system: The method is demonstrated on (1+1)-dimensional scalar phi-4 field theory and (2+1)-dimensional U(1) gauge theory in the dual formalism.
key_observables: Field truncation cutoffs (phi_max, pi_max, B_max), truncation error (epsilon), and expectation values of field operators.
important_parameters: Lattice volume (V), lattice spacing (a), mass (m_0), coupling constant (lambda_0 or g), and target digitization error (epsilon).
main_assumptions: The energy of the state relative to the vacuum is assumed to be below 1/a, and the method relies on the applicability of energy-based bounds.
figures_summary: Figure 12 and 13 show the extrapolation of field expectation values toward the continuum limit (zero temporal lattice spacing) for scalar and U(1) gauge theories.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of boson truncation error, reviews existing bounds, presents the new analytical and Monte Carlo-based methodologies, demonstrates the method's efficiency on scalar and gauge theories, and performs continuum limit extrapolations.
why_interesting: This work provides a more efficient way to digitize bosonic degrees of freedom, which is crucial for reducing the computational overhead and qubit requirements in the quantum simulation of gauge theories.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
